segment-text: Written by DAVID MAMET
A penitentiary, near the Canadian border. EXT. PRISON - NIGHT
Drifting snow. The camera cranes past a barbed wire fence, past the prison Penitentiary sign, past pacing guards with dogs on walkways, to reveal the prison beyond, searchlights flaring round it.
INT. PRISON - NIGHT

A mine, built into the mountainside, in which the prisoners are working. Camera reveals the various levels of the mine, a blur of steam, smoke, spurting gas-jets, the prisoners, covered in dust, working like moles against the rock-face. Guards with dogs pace on the walkways above. ANGLE - Two convicts, Ned and Jim, covered in fine dust, working a large vat. ANGLE - Tannoy speakers fixed to the walkways. The guards pacing. A siren sounds, and the prisoners cease work, begin to shuffle down to the bottom level. I - the Warden, a heavyset man with a riding crop, emerges through a cloud of steam onto the main walkway.
The taking of a life is no minor matter. (beat) Nor do we do it lightly. (beat) This man has killed, and he will be killed.
ANGLE - THE WARDEN, from below, over the heads of the prisoners, pacing above them.
Take him out. This is the end of crime, gentlemen. And it is called "punishment."
Two guards drag out a chained convict (BOBBY)

This man will be taken down, his head shaved; he will receive confession if he wishes it. We will then test the apparatus; the lights will dim, once, twice, the third time, then his soul will be in that New Place. I leave you to conjecture where that place will be.
Bobby being walked down the line of convicts, the CAMERA MOVES with him.
I want no demonstrations. I want no comments. I have no doubts but your thoughts will be troubled. Keep them to yourselves.
As he passes two convicts, NED and JIM:
So long, guys...
(sotto) So long, Bobby.
No talking.
(sotto) Hey, whaddya going to PS2 to him: Take away his commissary... Son of a bitch...
The CAMERA STAYS on Ned and Jim.
(sotto) Keep it down, Jim.
(sotto) Son of a bitch...
And there you have it, gentlemen. I wish you a good evening.
Bob being walked down the spiral staircase. speaks to his Assistant...
... Lock 'em up... The cell doors are opened; the convicts retire into their cells.
INT. NED AND JIM'S CELL
ANGLE - A "pretty girl" pin-up.
Can you believe it, can you believe it, Neddy...?
ANGLE - Ned, on his bunk, looks at the pin-up. lower bunk, smokes a cigarette.
I can believe most anything. My problem is I just, don't care.
They're taking Bobby to the bakery.
Sometimes it happens that way.
... that the guy's gonna die, and that's it...?
Hey, what the fuck, he's a murderer ... what*s your question...?
That, that's the end of it? That, that he goes in that room...

...and he goes in that room and they light him up and that's the end of it and there's no eternal life for his soul and the cruelty of the world... izzat the thing? Izzat your problem this fine evening...?
Jim, on the
Yes...
You wanna talk to the chaplain
Well, I'm talking to you...
I'm busy. I've got my mind oin highr things.
I feel bad, Ned.
Do your own time.
Ned pulls down the pin-up girl poster and looks at it. A GUARD appears at the cell door.
You, two...
He starts opening the door.- Ned and Jim look at each other * confusedly. The Guard throws in a length of chain.
... Put these on.
INT. PRISON CORRIDOR - NIGHT
Ned and Jim, manacled together, are marched down the corridor, the Guard behind them.
Just tell me what it is, just tell me what it is...
The Guard raps Jim in the kidneys with the stick.
Keep walking...
Ned looks at Jim.
INT. ELECTROCUTION COMPLEX - NIGHT
The electric chair; a Guard fussing over it. 11 ANGLE - Jim and Ned are walked into the anteroom by the Guard, look at the electric chair. The Guard pushes them down on the bench.
The Warden said, any man on the floor tonight gets chained, now shut it up, you hear me...?
As Ned and Jim sit in the anteroom, the Guard in the electric chair room makes a motion and the lights dim, go out for a moment then come back on.
(sotto to Jim) Life is a hellhole, pally.

Bob is walked through the anteroom by two Guards. He is attended by a PRIEST. He nods at Jim and Ned. He is walked across the anteroom. A door is opened. We see him seated and a barber throws a cloth over him and starts shaving his head. He looks over at Ned and Jim. They look on, terrified. The door to the barber room is closed. INTO THE PICTURES. Ned and Jim rise. The Warden comes
You two were talking. (beat) Answer me: you two were talking during my speech.
The Warden swipes at his face with his riding crop.
You sains... you swine.... (to his Guards) Hold him down...
Two Guards hold down Ned; two others turn Jim with his back to the Warden, rip Jim's shirt off of his back. 12 ANGLE - Jim, his face to the CAMERA, the Warden behind him, whipping him savagely.
You SHine., you want order? I'll give you order: I'll give you order! You want a lesson...? Is that what you want... M l
A Guard comes up to the Warden.
Warden, they're ready...
The Warden stops, turns to the Guard, motions at the electric chair, where there is a doctor, and several GUARDS standing by.
They' re ready, sir...
The Warden lowers his riding crop.
(to one of the Guards) Keep them here, we'll beat the other one afterwards.

The Warden turns, walks, followed by the other Guards, into the execution chamber. He closes the door after him. ANGLE - Ned and Jim alone on the bench, one Guard standing beyond them. Jim sobs, Ned tries to comfort him.
It's alright, it's alright, Jimmy...
Oh, Ned, I'm in bad shape...
And the Lord shall judge His people and repent Himself for His servants, when He seeth that their power is gone...
Ned lifts up his head. ANGLE - Bob, his head now shaved, being walked out of the Barber room, accompanied by the Priest and two Guards. Bob stops beside Ned and Jim. He looks ahead, transfixed.
And there is none shut up or left, and he shall say where are their gods, their rock, in whom they trusted...?

Ned hangs his head. The lights dim, flicker and go out. Beat. Several SHOTS ring out. The lights come back on. Bob is standing, holding a gun on a Guard. His two Guards are dead on the floor. Bob runs to the door of the execution chamber, and throws a massive bolt, preventing those beyond from entering.
Don't do this, my son...
Shut the hell up.
He hits the Priest on the side of the head with his gun. The Priest falls. Bob looks around, takes the doctor's smocks off a hook on the wall and throws them to Ned and Jim.
Here, put these on.
INT. PRISON CORRIDOR - NIGHT
Two doctors, seen from the back, pushing Bob on a trolley down the corridor. Guards rushing past them TOWARD THE CAMERA. One Guard turns to look at the doctors.
(back to CAMERA) We're taking him to the infirmary...
The Guard nods, rushes on.
15A ANGLE - The legs of the doctors manacled together.
15B ANGLE - Bob on the trolley, Ned and Jim, one in the doctor's shirt and collar and the other in his coat. Bob holds the revolver.
I'm not sure this is such a good idea.
I didn't ask you, Neddy.
Bob, they catch us now, they'll kill us.
Welcome aboard. f*(tm)'

The group comes to a walkway above the mine-complex, a huge stairway falling downwards. The siren begins to ring. Bob leaps out of the trolley. Guards are running up the stairs towards them. Bob shoots one. Ned and Jim are horrified, look at Bob. RLVISZZ'. L/l-t/:?
That's right; we're all in it now!

He shoves the trolley down the stairs towards the other guards, running up. ANGLE - The base of the stairs, guards falling backwards, under the impact of the trolley. The convicts escaping upwards, way above. ANGLE - Upper levels of mine. Bob running down a corridor, Ned and Jim behind him. He comes to a corner, where a large boiler is steaming. A guard rounds the corner and Bob pulls the boiler plug, encasing him in steam. POV - The Guard, screaming, flailing in the steam. ANGLE - Guards, seen through the gridded walkway at their feet, running, shooting up. ANGLE - Bobby, shooting through
the grids. He drags Ned
We going or we dying?
ANGLE - Upper levels of mine. The convicts running from the
flying bullets. Bob pulls open a metal door and we see the snow-storm raging outside against the face of the mountain, and a bottomless drop.
Bob...
You ever heard the phrase "nothing to lose..."?
He pushes them out, then jumps himself.
EXT. MINE - NIGHT
Ned and Jim, chained together, fall in deep snow. Bobby falls beside them. Bob rises, runs into the storm. Ned tries to drag Jim up.
Oh my God... Oh my God...
Searchlights pop on from the towers above them. Ned drags Jimmy through the snow to the base of the towers. They tumble down the slope, out of shot.
EXT. BASE OF PRISON - NIGHT

Ned and Jim tumble and slide down the mountainside. They burrow throught the snow, underneath the barbed wire fence. They run into the storm, as all the lights in the prison come on behind them. ANGLE - DOOR OF KENNELS. Dogs pouring out, snarling, charging, running to the hole in the fence, dragging guards with them. ANGLE - OUTSIDE THE FENCE. Dogs howling, the Warden and . guards blundering helplessly through the snowstorm.
EXT. SNOWY WOODS BRIDGE - DAY

ANGLE - a doe, seen on the bridge. Doe moves, we PAN with the doe, PAST a bundle on the ground under bridge. Doe moves OUT OF THE FRAME, PULL IN on the bundle which is Ned and Jim, huddled together shivering.
Wake up. Jim. (pause) Wake up.
Jim wakes up. They get up.
Come on. Come on, down across the river and we're free.
I'm cold.
Baby, I'm cold, too. Come on. Come on. Walk it out.
I'm cold.
They start to walk. ANGLE - their feet, chained together.
What happened to Bobby, do you think?
Uh huh.
Do you think they got 'im, Neddy?
Look at this...
EXT. SNOWCOVERED ROAD - DAY

stopped at the snowcovered road, looking at a poster. A picture of the "Weeping Virgin," and a huge arrow, and the legend "TWO MILES." "Patronize the local Brandon merchants. When in Brandon visit the famous Shrine of St. Ann, the Weeping Virgin." "Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers, for thereby some have entertained angels, unawares." Hebrews,
reading the sign.
Ned turns away. Jim reads.
( reads) ** "Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers, for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.
Very touching...
Hebrews...
Wait a second...!
Ned becomes very alert.
Listens. An old car
Get down!
Jim, roused from his reverie on the sign and the scripture, looks dreamily around. Ned pulls him down. crouched down in the snow. Ned looks up. An old car coming down the road.

looking, something else catches his eyes. But now the doe we saw previously has wandered out into the road. ANGLE - NED AND JIM Both looking. The OLD WOMAN driving, looks up. ANGLE - HER POV - THE DOE looking huge through the windshield.
feet try to run on the icy surface of the road. ANGLE - NED AND JIM look away. The SOUND of a huge crash. look up. Pause. Ned and Jim ANGLE - NED AND JIM walking over to the car.
ANGLE - THE CAR
The dying doe under its wheels. The Old Woman behind the wheel. Ned and Jim walk over to her. Pause.
Hello...!
I'm alright, I'm alright. out of here.
They help her out of the car.
- Ned looks a t s o m e t h i n g . . . An old side by side shotgun on the back seat.
looking down at the deer.
We're looking for a ride into town. Are you, indeed, why should I give you one, I don't know who the hell you are...
We*re going down to town to see the Shrine. You prob'ly scared the damn deer out into the road. What're you doing in the woods? Who are you?
Pause.
"Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers for, thereby, some have entertained angels unawares.
looking at them. The dark coats over the doctor's smocks make them look like they are wearing clerical collars. "WE'RE NO ANGELS" ANGLE - THE THREE
That's right. Well, you help me move the deer, I'll give you a ride. (The car starts. . .)
The Old Woman goes back into the car, comes out with the shotgun, hands it to Ned.

CLOSEUP - Jim looking at the buck. POV - THE BUCK'S HEAD large, terrified eyes. CLOSEUP - Jim looking down. He looks to the side. Ned points the shotgun at the buck, fires. Then he quickly points the barrel at the chains between their legs, fires again. The chain shatters. NED LOOKS AT JIM.
(sotto) Whatta we going to do about the old broad?
She thinks we're priests.
He hands the shotgun back to her and she puts it in the gun rack.
(sotto to Jim) Yeah, we brought a little religion into that broad's life...
"WE'RE NO ANGELS" INT. CAR/COUNTRY ROAD - DAY
Ned, with a bandana, trying to clean the blood off his clothes, Jim beside him.
(sotto) . . . into the town, across the bridge and we're free. I'm sure they're looking for you.
Ned turns to her.
What...?
I'm sure t h e y ' r e looking f o r you.
Who's looking for us? At the monastery. Your fellow priests.
Yes, I'm sure they're looking for us. What are you, missing morning prayers?
Yeah, we're missing the whole program, what do you care...?
I don't care a damn thing, I tole you, it's all superstition, far as I'm concerned.
That's fine. Now you just drop us in the town... And I'll tell you what else, get people worked up...
She turns to the back seat to talk to them.
shrine of yours... "The Weeping
46 * EXT. TOWN - DAY
ANGLE - The town which they are just entering, coming down a hill, a small bridge, two border checkpoints on either end. 47 ANGLE - The three passengers in the car.
We'll get out here.
Won't be one more minute, I'll have you down.
No, we'll get out here.
Well, I could use your help, carry my deer to the butcher.
She stops the car.
I tell you what, you do down, we'll meet you at the garage, carry your deer for you. Why'nt you just come down with me?
I think we need a moment to compose our thoughts.
'Weren't for you I never'd*a killed that deer.
Uh huh. Well, thank you for your courtesy. God bless you, and all that.
Ned and Jim get out of the car.
EXT. TOWN SQUARE - DAY
The doe seen in the back, the two convicts get out. car drives off. They look across the river.
EXT. SHANTY TOWN - RIVERBANK - DAY
THEIR POV - THE BRIDGE The* far side of the river. ANGLE - The two convicts.
Can you swim, Jimmy?
NO. Me neither.
Come on.
CAMERA FOLLOWS them down the hill through the beginning of the small town. Ned stops in front of a window, looks at his reflection.
You think Bob made it?
Not looking like this he didn't we're covered in blood... Come on, we got to ditch these togs.
EXT. SHANTY TOWN - BOARDWALK - DAY
CAMERA FOLLOWS them around to the back of the house. of laundry stretch from the houses before them. Rows
Whatdaya gonna do on Free Street, Neddy?
I'm gonna do it all, pal. (beat) And then I'm gonna do it again -- and I'm gonna get two the best of everything -- and anybody muck with me is going down.

CAMERA FOLLOWS them as they start taking the laundry off the various lines. They hungrily dress themselves in the warm clothes. Ned moves on, dressing himself, O.S. Jimmy stops, looking at something. A mother is serving breakfast to two little kids, hugging them. CLOSEUP - JIM'S FACE wistful. ANGLE - Ned moves down the line, dressing himself. down a shirt, sees something beyond it.
POV - ANOTHER WINDOW
He takes A beautiful naked young woman, heating water at her stove, pours it into her washbowl. She ties her hair up with a large yellow ribbon, starts washing her face. This is MOLLY.
Maan, what I wouldn't give, what I wouldn*t give...
ANGLE - Ned looks at the naked young woman. Jim looks in the opposite direction, putting on a vest he has just taken down from the line.
What I wouldn't give for a cigarette...
(turning away) Let's get out of here...
EXT. TOWN SQUARE - ALLEY - DAY The two men move down Main St. to Alley between hotel and
. barber shop. Ned plucks down a heavy woolen overshirt. They stop. Beyond them, traffic just starting to move. They stand in the shade of the alley. A monk driving a tractor passes by...
You ready...?
I wonder what happened to Bob, I
wonder did he get across...
What happened to him happened to him, Jim.
Maybe they got him back; but I'm not going back there.
That's right. We're not going back.
We look, hey, we look like a couple of hoosiers...
That's right. Let's go.

They start out into the street. Jim sees something, motions Ned to stop. He scampers up a short staircase and puts on a heavy woolen coat hanging on a peg outside shopkeeper's back door. He comes down.
Okay; let*s go...
As they walk out, they pass under a banner across the street. It reads "CELEBRATE THE FEAST OF OUR LADY OF THE SORROWS."
EXT. TOWN SQUARE - DAY
The two convicts come out onto the street. purposefully forward. They walk THEIR POV - THE BRIDGE up ahead, the customs shed, the barrier.
ANGLE - The convicts.
Jim starts to whistle. He jingles
(producing coins from his pocket) Hey, coins in the pocket, that's good luck. That's good luck, don't you think...?
Jimmy sees something before him. walking over to the customs shed, distributing leaflets.
Oh, God, oh, God...
ANGLE - Jimmy and Ned
It's okay, it's going to be okay. A drayman is coming up the street in his horse-drawntruck.
He CRACKS the whip over the horse. Jim winces, his hand goes to rub his back.
I'm not going back, Ned.
Nobody's going back. be fine.
It's going to Jim wipes his forehead.
I need a smoke, Ned, I'm telling you...
Okay. We'll get you a smoke, and then we go across. You'll take care of me, Ned, 'cause I got to get across.
Just keep walking...
They veer off in their progress toward the bridge. CAMERA FOLLOWS them to the sidewall, to a general store. The owner, inside, is just seen turning the "Closed" sign to "Open."
INT. GENERAL STORE - DAY
Ned and Jim enter. customer. The SHOPKEEPER is in the back with a
(looking up) Right with you.
Jim, looking nervous, nudges Ned. Ned looks. tacked on the wall beind a display, with three crudely drawn faces"on it. The same poster the Sheriff was distributing
ANGLE - Ned and Jim.
(to Shopkeeper) Just a pack of tailormades...
Right with you.
Jim looks out the window. POV - THE BORDER CROSSING The Sheriff's Deputies look intently at people going across. ANGLE - Jim turns back to the store. something catches their interest.
POV - THE DISPLAY COUNTER
He and Ned look down, full of handguns, a "Colt" display. ANGLE - Jim looks at Ned, catches his eye, then looks at the guns again.
Buncha nonsense, all them damn priests in towns, the Shrine, don't buy a damn thing, but they want something, you better have it on hand... I'll be with you gents in a second.
We just want a packet of smokes...

Jim turns his back on the Shopkeeper, slides toward the guns. He picks up a promotional pamphlet off the top of the counter. Holds it up. It reads "Colt On The Trail." It has pictures of revolvers on the cover. He opens it, reads, "Have you ever been completely alone, with no one to rely on...?"
Mementoes of the Shrine. postcards...
ANGLE - The Shopkeeper opens a carton of souvenirs. holds one up.
Key chains of the Weeping Virgin... not a penny in it, but they want it every year, priests and the tourists... Put on that Dumb Show.
SOUND of the shopbell ringing. Molly, the girl with the yellow ribbon in her hair, enters the store. Walks past Ned, over to the counter. She picks up two containers of oatmeal.
Account's gettin' a bit long...
Well, I need it, set it down.
I got a hungry kid. You'll catch me later.
She picks up the two packages, starts out of the store. is looking at her.
What're vou looking at?
He holds up his hands, i.e., "No offense..."
She exits.
(to Ned) Special kind of smokes?
He turns back.
Camel's'11 do...
The Shrine, talking about it. Didn't mean to offend you, now 'n' I probably put my foot in it n*you're tourists, come to see the Shrine...
Beat. CAMERA PANS WITH the Shopkeeper as he turns to the cigarette display, picks a pack of smokes out of it, and stops. Tacked next to the cigarette display is the "wanted" poster. Beat.
... Where are you folks from...?
The Shopkeeper turns with the smokes to Ned, Jim is in the background, with the pamphlet, trying to reach behind to steal a gun.
Oh, here and there...
(to Jim) Something I can interest you in that case...?
He walks over to Jim.
Oh, we should be 'bout going... Ned pays for the smokes.
(to Jim) ... We should be 'bout going now...
Jim folds the pamphlet, puts it into his coat, they start to edge toward the door.
You forgot your change.
Oh, that's okay...
At the door they freeze. . outside the door, talking to his Deputy. ANGLE - The two turning back toward the Shopkeeper.
Yessir, change. Lessee if we can't spend it in your store.
The Shopkeeper hands the change to Ned.
You folks hear about the jailbreak...?
No, I can't say that we have.
Pause. The Shopkeeper looks at them. He looks at the
Where'd you say you were from...? f*"* .

CLOSEUP - NED he looks outside. The Sheriff is right outside the door. ANGLE - Ned looks at Jim. Jim starts to edge toward the guncase. ANGLE - The Shopkeeper behind the counter, edges toward a pistol laying on a low shelf.
Yessir, it seems there were these three convicts... shot their way out...
He starts to pick up the gun. We hear DOORBELL JINGLING.
ANGLE - The old Woman has just come into the shop. I told you I needed your help.

... my help...? My car's stalled, down at the garage, n'l need you to haul that deer down to the butchers.
You know these folks?
Know 'em, yeah, they're priests. This is Father... I didn't catch * your name...
Priests? Oh, God, oh, God, I'm sorry. I'm sorry, what I'm saying ... gentlemen, Father, ramblin' on, and now I've offended you. No, no, no hard "feelings"...
That's fine.
You going to help me with that deer...?
I mean, I'die f'l thought I offended you...
No, we'll forget it.
Or anyone associated with the Shrine...
He comes around the corner, picks up handfuls of "shrine" key chains, and presses them on Ned and Jim.
W e l l , h e r e , little t o k e n . T a k e
It shows the "Shrine of the Weeping Virgin." over in his hand and we see the thermometer. Ned turns it ANGLE - The Old Woman, ushering the two convicts out the door.
Awfully, handy, really, (he glances at the thermometer) Cool day today! Button up!...
He waves.
Yes, indeed.
(smiling) No hard feelings... Father... Go with God.
(pointing at Jim) I have that same coat!
They all smile at each other cheerily. The Old Woman turns back to the Shopkeeper, hands him a piece of paper. Harry: here's a list, and I'll

The three walking down the street. Damn car's all stove in, need a new radiator, how'm I going to afford

They pass by.a wanted poster, showing the faces of the three wanted convicts. They come up to the garage. In the background, the car, with the deer in it. ... Cheaper to shop in Canada...

I walk over, how'm I going to get

POV - SEVERAL PEOPLE
lined up to get across, being interrogated by the DEPUTY. ANGLE - Ned, Jim, The Old Woman.
After what you've done for us, we'll go with you and fetch your things back.
They change their course and walk toward the border.
Well, you know what it says in the Bible... What's that?
You know...
Matter of fact, I usually do my shopping over there, though I wouldn't let on to Harry. You think that's dishonestly, you just go on and think it...
They draw closer to the border. Jim begins to look afraid. He leans down to hitch up his leg iron, the chains CLINK as he does it.
(sotto, to Ned) I can't do it.
(sotto) Yes, you can, Jim. cake.
It's a piece of
(sotto) I can't do it.

(sotto) Now, you want to kill yourself, that's fine. But there's two of us here, n'I'm counting on you, so you buck up, Jimmy, n'you act like a priest. They look toward the border. checking faces against the wanted poster.
takes the key chain out of his pocket.
(very softly) ... Hail Mary, Full of Grace...

82 ANGLE - Jim watches the Deputy walk the Old Woman toward the

HIS POV 84 ANGLE - Ned hangs his head.
